title: elaeocarpus hylobroma
intro: Elaeocarpus hylobroma, also known as the Malabar olive tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves and fruits of the Elaeocarpus hylobroma have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of Elaeocarpus hylobroma include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed as teas or added to traditional remedies.
